title: ophioglossum capense var. nudicaule
intro: Ophioglossum capense var. nudicaule, also known as the Naked or Nudicaule Adder's Tongue, is a variety of the Ophioglossum capense plant, a type of fern that belongs to the Ophioglossaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ophioglossum capense var. nudicaule include treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and insect bites. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy by drying and powdering the rhizomes, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or topical ointments.
